title: populus fremontii subsp. fremontii
intro: Populus fremontii subsp. fremontii, also known as the California cottonwood or Fremont cottonwood, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Populus fremontii subsp. fremontii include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
